decisions.decision: Target A: Guided Intake and Collection (Steps 2-6)
decisions.reasoning: User confirmed recommended target. Highest score (23/25). Tests the hardest behavioral assumption first — will clients submit through a structured system?

decisions.decision: Solution 1: The Interview — Conversational Guided Intake Flow, with Solution 3 (The Head Start) web-scraping pre-population integrated
decisions.reasoning: User accepted recommendation. Highest-scoring scalable solution (53/65). Structured guidance solves root cause of client non-submission. Web scraping pre-population from Head Start reduces client burden and adds 'we did our homework' delight.

decisions.decision: Target A + C Hybrid — Network-Wide Digital Modernization Pipeline with Practice-Facing Agent Interface. Stage 2 (future sprint): Target B — 365-Day Curation Engine.
decisions.reasoning: User confirmed all three targets matter but agreed on staged approach. A + C combined because the agent IS the onboarding interface. Critical reframe: this is not just new acquisition onboarding — the system must re-enroll all 260+ existing practices to modernize the entire network. Stage 2 (365-day curation) depends on Stage 1 establishing baseline storefronts.

decisions.decision: Solution 1: The Practice Concierge — Agent-First Orchestration Platform, enhanced with Assembly Line blitz-deploy for baseline practices and Practice Playbook guardrailed decisions pattern.
decisions.reasoning: User accepted recommendation. Highest-scoring solution (30/35). The agent-first architecture is the only solution that solves setup speed AND establishes the ongoing relationship needed for Stage 2 (365-day curation). Matches SGA's vision of chat today → voice → video avatar. Enhancements: blitz-deploy defaults for bottom 20% (from Assembly Line), guardrailed decision pattern (from Practice Playbook). Evolutionary path: MVP Month 1, full integrations Month 2-3, curation layer Month 3-6, potential Network OS backend Month 6-12.

decisions.decision: All Three Targets Combined — Data Integration Layer (A) + Executive Visibility Dashboard (B) prioritized first, with Practice Nerve Center (C) fully architected as Stage 2. Full architecture for all three in the deliverable.
decisions.reasoning: User wants the complete system architected so they can see how C (Practice Nerve Center) gets executed. A+B first because the data layer and executive dashboard are foundational — you can't build a practice interface without the data flowing. C is the highest-impact piece for practice staff but depends on A+B being established.

decisions.decision: Solution 1: The Funnel OS — Full-Stack Unified Platform with staged delivery. Stage 1 uses off-the-shelf BI tool for quick exec dashboard while platform is built. Practice Nerve Center extends Practice Concierge agent pattern from Storefront sprint.
decisions.reasoning: User accepted recommendation. Highest-scoring solution (28/35). Only solution where all three layers (integration, dashboard, nerve center) are native to a single coherent platform. Staged delivery addresses time-to-value weakness: Month 1 BI dashboard, Month 2-3 AI analysis, Month 3-5 Practice Nerve Center, Month 5-8 full automation + network rollout. Long-term convergence with Practice Concierge from Storefront sprint.
